mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, consumer spending power deteriorates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.01 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during expansion periods, with a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect labor market tightening and rising wage income, which consistently generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns in >55% of months, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 260.0, inflation expectations rise and real discount rates compress, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as monetary policy tightens.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 260.0 are followed by positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample models.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index signal manufacturing contraction and weakening supply-side momentum, which cause negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during recessionary periods.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during recessions, with a positive Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.
